title: Ptychotis involucrata
intro: Ptychotis involucrata, also known as the Pyrenean forget-me-not or Pyrenean chamomile, is a flowering plant species in the family Apiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from anxiety, insomnia, and gastrointestinal issues due to its calming effects, which have been attributed to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Ptychotis involucrata include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas and infusions, as well as essential oil extracts used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.

The species Ptychotis involucrata is native to certain parts of the world, such as the Iberian Peninsula.
